stories.headline: US launches air strikes as Iran targets tankers with cruise missiles
stories.summary: Donald Trump demands 20% fee on cargoes passing through Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: US military targeted in Iran war phone-tracking campaign
stories.summary: Roaming systems and ad tech were used to try to locate American personnel as Tehran attacked forces in the region

stories.headline: US bombs Iran again, Tehran strikes Gulf, tankers: What’s the latest?
stories.summary: While Trump has threatened to hit targets in Iran including Pickaxe Mountain, he has said a deal is possible.

stories.headline: US military releases video of its latest strikes on Iran
stories.summary: US Central Command has released video showing its latest wave of attacks reportedly hitting Iran's mainland and islands.

stories.headline: UAE condemns Iran's 'brazen' attack on tankers as US launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: The third consecutive night of US strikes on Iran came as Trump announced a 20% charge as part of a new blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: SoftBank’s Son ridicules AI critics for ‘spitting upwards’
stories.summary: Business leaders unwilling to embrace technology ‘should play the role of spouse’, says controversial investor

stories.headline: Asian Oil Buyers Look to US Crude as Iran War Intensifies
stories.summary: The escalation of hostilities between Washington and Tehran and the virtual standstill in observable traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is prompting Asian refiners to look at buying more US oil.

stories.headline: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls
stories.summary: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.

stories.headline: Targeting Patriarch Kirill risks fuelling EU scepticism, Bulgarian foreign minister warns
stories.summary: Velislava Petrova-Chamova told Euronews that the EU’s sanctions against Russia should have a financial impact and not be a symbolic instrument – and that this, among other reasons, is why Sofia championed the removal of the head of Russia's Orthodox Church from the 21st sanctions package.

stories.headline: China's June exports surge 27% from a year earlier as AI boom drives strong demand
stories.summary: China’s exports accelerated in June, jumping 27% from a year earlier thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence, the customs agency said on Tuesday.

stories.headline: China’s monthly car exports surge past 1mn for first time
stories.summary: Overall trade soars as Beijing says it is also world’s second-largest importer

stories.headline: BofA Warns Stock Bulls to Consider Curbing Aggressive Buying
stories.summary: Global investors buying stocks aggressively should consider reducing exposure, according to Bank of America Corp.’s fund manager survey.

stories.headline: BP Flags $1 Billion Writedown From Low Carbon Business
stories.summary: BP Plc said it expects to write down another $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter, as the British major continues the painstaking work of re-orientating itself toward its core oil and gas business.

stories.headline: Warsh to Move Markets More Than He Wants: 3-Minutes MLIV
stories.summary: Anna Edwards, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Middle East War Set to Push Back LNG Glut by a Year, BNEF Says
stories.summary: The liquefied natural gas market will move into oversupply in 2028, a year later than previously forecast, as war in the Middle East and major project delays push back the emergence of a global glut, according to BloombergNEF.

stories.headline: Brazil’s First Ethanol-Powered Ship Sails in Win for Biofuels
stories.summary: The first container ship to run on Brazilian-made ethanol fuel is scheduled to sail early Tuesday from the port of Santos, an unprecedented test for the nation’s biofuel industry that marks a key step toward cutting emissions in maritime transportation.

stories.headline: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
stories.summary: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.

stories.headline: Oil prices hit 1-month high as US-Iran attacks dim Strait of Hormuz outlook
stories.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran.

stories.headline: Hungary’s parliament votes to oust president in latest anti-Orban move
stories.summary: Hungarian parliament passes amendment that would remove President Sulyok, appointed under ex-Prime Minister Orban.

stories.headline: Record El Niño threatens to unleash floods across East Africa and Asia
stories.summary: Aid groups warn a record-strength El Niño could unleash flooding and hunger from Somalia to Pakistan.

stories.summary: Monthly indicators show weakness in retail sales and investment ahead of second-quarter figure

stories.headline: India’s Birla Taps MUFG for $1.5 Billion to Fund Shell Asset Buy
stories.summary: A unit of billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla’s conglomerate has lined up around $1.5 billion of acquisition financing from Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. to fund its purchase of Shell Plc’s renewable energy assets in the country, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: China Widens Australian Canola Trade to Include Private Crushers
stories.summary: China will allow its private oilseed crushers to buy Australian canola, as the two countries move to normalize a trade that’s largely been halted since the start of the decade.

stories.headline: Rathbones Cut Gilt Exposure in Case Burnham ‘Does a Truss’
stories.summary: Rathbones Asset Management has reduced its holdings of UK government bonds to avoid a potential selloff if Andy Burnham boosts spending and raises borrowing.

stories.headline: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
stories.summary: Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of ‘buy now, pay later loans’, in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.

stories.headline: Europe Is Set for the Strongest Earnings Growth in Three Years
stories.summary: European companies are set to post their strongest profit growth in three years in the second-quarter reporting season, buoyed by expected blowout results from oil majors, banks and artificial intelligence winners.

stories.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/14/2026
stories.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank and World Bank Team Up for Trade Finance Platform
stories.summary: The World Bank and Deutsche Bank AG are starting a €1 billion trade finance platform for frontier and emerging markets.

stories.headline: Improving Hiring Trends Boost Embattled European Recruiters
stories.summary: European staffing companies’ better-than-feared numbers and cautiously optimistic outlook are pointing to a stabilizing labor market after years of downturn.

stories.headline: US-Iran Clashes Lift Oil, Rate Hike Bets | Insight with Haslinda Amin 7/14/2026
stories.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
stories.summary: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
stories.summary: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to serve the rest of his Senate term
stories.summary: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) announced his selection of Darline Graham Nordone to serve into January. She is expected to be sworn in Tuesday.

stories.headline: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Death Transforms the Race for His Democratic Challenger
stories.summary: Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, has offered condolences to those who loved Mr. Graham while considering the political fallout from his death. Republicans are scrambling to arrange a new primary election.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Sister Appointed to Finish His Senate Term After Trump’s Backing
stories.summary: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina named Darline Graham Nordone to replace Mr. Graham, who died on Saturday.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants
stories.summary: High temperatures and below average rainfall put pressure on waterways used to cool reactorsAbove average temperatures combined with below average rainfall across much of western and central Europe during June and the first half of July have placed increasing pressure on rivers, ecosystems and energy infrastructure. Persistent high pressure brought prolonged sunshine, suppressed rainfall and enhanced evaporation, causing river levels to fall and

stories.headline: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

stories.headline: Volkswagen planning to cut up to 100,000 jobs globally
stories.summary: The group, which includes Porsche and Audi, has faced a steep fall in profits and tough competition from China.

stories.headline: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
stories.summary: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".

stories.headline: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister

stories.headline: US and Iran trade fire as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: UK and Switzerland to loosen visa restrictions as part of services trade deal
stories.summary: Agreement will also enable British travellers to use automated passport gates at Swiss airports

stories.headline: Africa’s $219 Billion Fund Suspends CEO as Internal Rift Deepens
stories.summary: The board of Africa’s largest state-run fund suspended Chief Executive Officer Patrick Dlamini in a late-night move that highlighted the deepening feud at the $219 billion asset manager.

stories.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

stories.headline: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall

stories.headline: South Africa says more than 53,000 foreigners deported in migration campaign
stories.summary: South Africa is cracking down on undocumented migrants following widespread anti-immigration protests.

stories.headline: Small plane crash in Bahamas kills 10 people
stories.summary: The country's prime minister called the crash, on the country's independence day, a tragedy.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
stories.summary: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. stories.headline: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
stories.summary: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

stories.headline: Motherhood not all warm and fuzzy: Bollywood actor's play spotlights messier side
stories.summary: As a society, we take mothers for granted and raising children is a thankless job, says actor Kalki Koechlin.

stories.headline: Huge crowds in Mashhad as Iran's late supreme leader is buried
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei was buried at the Imam Reza shrine, Iran's holiest Shia Muslim site, ending six days of public mourning ceremonies.

stories.headline: Big fall in oil, gas and cargo ships taking US-backed Hormuz route after new strikes
stories.summary: Data shows a decline in the number of ships - many carrying oil and gas - going through the waterway after attacks this week.

stories.headline: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran as Tehran warns of 'fearless' response
stories.summary: Speaking at a Nato summit, the president says the US will "probably" strike Iran again but added that "anything that happens will be over quickly".

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: Are Argentina being treated favourably at World Cup?
stories.summary: Egypt have alleged bias in favour of Argentina and favouritism towards their star player, Lionel Messi. Is this fair?

stories.headline: Khamenei's coffin carried through Shia shrines as ceremonies held in Iraq
stories.summary: The remains of Iran's late supreme leader are transported to Najaf and Karbala, as funeral ceremonies continue for a fifth day.

stories.summary: For the world’s largest companies, the normal rules of corporate governance no longer apply
